metadata.website_type: wedding planning tips and inspiration blog

tasks.name: Save 3 budget wedding articles under $10,000 from the last 12 months
tasks.description: Find budget-planning articles for weddings under $10,000 that were published in the last 12 months, sort them by popularity, and save exactly 3 of them to your reading list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the “Budget & Money” category in the top navigation menu.
In the sidebar filters, click the “Overall Budget” dropdown and select the option labeled “Under $10,000”.
In the same sidebar, click the “Published date” filter and select “Last 12 months”.
Use the sort dropdown above the article list and select “Most Popular”.
Click the title of the first article in the sorted results list to open its article page.
On the article page, click the “Save to reading list” button located near the article title, then use the browser back button to return to the filtered results.

Repeat the previous two actions for the second and third articles in the results list so that you have saved the first three under-$10,000 budget articles from the last 12 months to your reading list.

tasks.name: Save an 8-hour summer outdoor wedding day timeline for 50–150 guests with a 4 PM ceremony
tasks.description: Use the timeline tool to configure a wedding day schedule for a summer outdoor wedding with 50–150 guests, an 8-hour event, and a 4:00 PM ceremony, then save that specific timeline template to your planning board.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the “Planning Tools” option in the top navigation and then select “Timeline Templates” from the dropdown menu.
In the “Guest Count” filter, select the range labeled “50–150 guests”.
In the “Season” dropdown filter, select “Summer”.
In the “Location Type” filter, check the option labeled “Outdoor ceremony & reception”.
In the “Timeline Length” filter, select the “8 hours” option.
In the “Ceremony Time” dropdown, choose “4:00 PM”.
From the resulting list of timeline templates, click the first template whose title or label includes “Summer Outdoor” and “4 PM Ceremony” to open its detail page.
On the template page, click the “Save this timeline” button to add this 8-hour summer outdoor 4 PM ceremony timeline to your planning board.

tasks.name: Create a moodboard with 4 sage green and gold color palettes that include neutrals
tasks.description: Filter color palette inspiration by tags and palette type to find sage green and gold palettes that include neutral tones, then create a moodboard named “Sage & Gold” and add exactly 4 such palettes to it.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the “Inspiration” item in the top navigation, then select the “Color Palettes” subcategory.
In the tag filter area, click the “Sage Green” tag and also click the “Gold” tag so that both tags are active filters.
In the “Palette Type” filter, select the option labeled “Includes Neutrals”.
Click the “Create New Moodboard” button near the top of the page, and when prompted for a name, type “Sage & Gold” and confirm.
Scroll to the filtered list of color palettes and locate the first four palette cards that visibly show both green and gold swatches in their preview.
For each of those four palette cards, click the “Add to moodboard” or “+” icon and choose the “Sage & Gold” moodboard from the list of moodboards.
Click the “My Moodboards” link in the header and then click on the “Sage & Gold” moodboard to open it and view the four added color palettes.

tasks.name: Save one venue guide for ~150 guests that mentions a backup indoor space
tasks.description: Within the venue guides, compare barn and hotel ballroom articles suitable for around 150 guests and save one article that explicitly mentions a backup indoor space to your reading list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the “Venues” category in the top navigation menu.
In the “Guest Count” filter, select the option labeled “Around 150 guests (120–180)”.
In the “Venue Style” filter, check both “Barn” and “Hotel Ballroom” so that articles about these two styles are shown.
From the results list, click the first article whose title includes the word “Barn” to open its article page.

Scroll through the article’s subheadings; if you find a subheading that includes the phrase “backup indoor space”, click the “Save” or bookmark icon near the article title, then proceed to the next step.
Use the browser back button to return to the venue results list, then click the first article whose title includes “Ballroom” or “Hotel Ballroom” to open it.
If this ballroom article has a subheading that contains the phrase “backup indoor space”, click the “Save” or bookmark icon near its title.

Click the “Saved” or “Reading List” link in the site header to open the list of saved items, which now includes at least one venue article suitable for around 150 guests that mentions a backup indoor space.

tasks.name: Save a seating layout template for 100–150 guests using mixed round and long tables
tasks.description: Find a seating and floorplan article for a guest count of 100–150 that uses both round and long tables, view its sample chart, and save that layout to your planning board.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the “Planning” option in the top navigation and select “Seating & Floorplans” from the dropdown.
In the “Guest Count” filter, choose the “100–150 guests” option.
In the “Layout Type” filters, check both “Round tables” and “Long tables” so that layouts using both shapes are included.
Use the sort dropdown above the layouts and select “Most Used” or “Most Downloaded”.
From the top of the sorted list, click the first article whose summary or title mentions “mixed round and long tables” to open it.
On the article page, click the “View sample seating chart” button to open the detailed layout view.
Click the “Save layout” or “Add this layout to my planning board” button to store this mixed-table layout for 100–150 guests in your planning board.

tasks.name: Generate a 6-month wedding checklist for under $15,000 and add 5 tasks to your to-dos
tasks.description: Use the checklist tool to create a 6-month planning checklist for a wedding with a budget under $15,000 and 80–120 guests, then add the first 5 tasks from the current month’s section to your to-do list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the “Checklists” item in the top navigation.
In the “Timeline” filter, select the option labeled “6 months”.
In the “Budget” filter, choose the option “Under $15,000”.
In the “Guest Count” filter, select the range “80–120 guests”.
Click the “Generate checklist” or “View checklist” button to display the customized 6-month planning checklist.

Within the checklist, locate the section labeled “This Month” (or the first month in the 6-month timeline) and click the “Add to my to-dos” button or checkbox for the first five tasks listed in that section.
Click the “My To-Dos” or “Task List” icon in the header to open your centralized to-do list, which now contains those five checklist tasks.

tasks.name: Add a catering budget article under $60 per guest to your budget planner
tasks.description: Within the food and drink section, filter for catering budget breakdowns of $40–$60 per guest, review the first two articles, and add at least one article that lists an average cost of $60 or less per guest to your budget planner.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the “Food & Drink” category in the top navigation.
In the “Budget per Guest” filter, select the range “$40–$60”.
Use the sort dropdown above the article list and choose the option labeled “Budget breakdowns first” or similar.
Click the title of the first article in the filtered results to open its article page.
In this article, locate the catering budget table; if it lists an average total catering cost of $60 or less per guest, click the “Add to budget plan” button on the article page.

Use the browser back button to return to the results list, then click the second article in the filtered results to open it and, if its budget table also shows an average cost of $60 or less per guest, click the “Add to budget plan” button there as well.
Click the “Budget Planner” link or icon in the header to open the planner view, where the “Catering” section now includes at least one article reference you added for catering under $60 per guest.

tasks.name: Save 3 DIY decor projects under 2 hours and under $100 to your project list
tasks.description: Filter DIY decor tutorials by time and cost so they can be completed in under 2 hours and cost less than $100 each, then add exactly 3 such projects to your project list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the “DIY & Decor” category in the top navigation.
In the “Time Required” filter, select the option labeled “Under 2 hours”.
In the “Project Cost” filter, select the option “Under $100”.
In the “Difficulty” filter, choose “Beginner” to narrow the results to simpler projects.
Scroll to the filtered list of DIY projects and, starting from the top, click the first project to open its tutorial page and then click the “Add to project list” button for that project.

Use the browser back button to return to the DIY project list and repeat the previous step for the next two projects in the list, so that you add a total of three different under-2-hour, under-$100 projects to your project list.
Click the “My Projects” link or icon in the header to open your project list, which now displays those three selected DIY decor tutorials.

entities.name: Article
entities.description: Editorial articles such as budget guides, venue guides, and food & drink budget breakdowns.

fields.name: category_id
fields.description: Matches URL param categoryId (e.g., budget_money, venues, food_drink).

fields.name: popularity_score
fields.description: Used for sorting by Most Popular.

fields.name: overall_budget_min
fields.description: Minimum total wedding budget this article is aimed at.

fields.name: overall_budget_max
fields.description: Maximum total wedding budget this article is aimed at (e.g., 10000 for 'Under $10,000').

fields.name: overall_budget_label
fields.description: Display label for overall budget range (e.g., 'Under $10,000').

fields.name: guest_count_min
fields.description: Minimum recommended guest count (e.g., 120).

fields.name: guest_count_max
fields.description: Maximum recommended guest count (e.g., 180).

fields.name: guest_count_label
fields.description: Display label for guest count range (e.g., 'Around 150 guests (120–180)').

fields.name: venue_style_tags
fields.description: Array of venue style slugs such as 'barn', 'hotel_ballroom', etc.

fields.name: budget_per_guest_min
fields.description: Minimum per-guest budget in dollars (e.g., 40).

fields.name: budget_per_guest_max
fields.description: Maximum per-guest budget in dollars (e.g., 60).

fields.name: budget_per_guest_label
fields.description: Display label for per-guest budget range (e.g., '$40–$60').

fields.name: is_budget_breakdown
fields.description: True if this is a detailed budget breakdown article (used for 'Budget breakdowns first' sorting).

fields.name: average_catering_cost_per_guest
fields.description: Average total catering cost per guest in dollars, used to check <= $60.

fields.name: has_backup_indoor_space
fields.description: True if the article explicitly mentions a backup indoor space (venue guides).

fields.name: tags
fields.description: Free-form tags for search and filtering.

fields.name: read_time_minutes
fields.description: Estimated reading time.

entities.name: SavedArticle
entities.description: Articles saved to the Reading List for later reading.

fields.name: saved_at
fields.description: When the article was added to the reading list.

fields.name: note
fields.description: Optional user note about why it was saved.

entities.name: TimelineTemplate
entities.description: Wedding day timeline templates with filters for guest count, season, location, length, and ceremony time.

fields.name: guest_count_label
fields.description: Display label like '50–150 guests'.

fields.name: length_hours
fields.description: Total event length in hours (e.g., 8).

fields.name: ceremony_time
fields.description: Ceremony start time in display format such as '4:00 PM'.

fields.name: popularity_score
fields.description: Used for sorting by Most Popular.

fields.name: schedule
fields.description: Array of timeline blocks (time, label, description, etc.).

entities.name: SeatingLayout
entities.description: Seating and floorplan layout templates, including mixed round and long table layouts.

fields.name: guest_count_label
fields.description: Display label like '100–150 guests'.

fields.name: layout_type_options
fields.description: Array of table layout type slugs such as 'round_tables', 'long_tables'.

fields.name: is_mixed_tables
fields.description: True if the layout uses both round and long tables.

fields.name: popularity_score
fields.description: Generic popularity metric.

fields.name: usage_count
fields.description: Used for 'Most Used' sorting.

fields.name: download_count
fields.description: Used for 'Most Downloaded' sorting.

fields.name: sample_chart_image
fields.description: URL to the sample seating chart image or detailed view.

fields.name: table_summary
fields.description: Short summary mentioning e.g. 'mixed round and long tables'.

entities.name: PlanningBoardItem
entities.description: Items saved to the Planning Board, such as timeline templates and seating layouts.

fields.name: item_id
fields.description: ID of the saved TimelineTemplate or SeatingLayout.

entities.name: DIYProject
entities.description: DIY & decor project tutorials with time, cost, and difficulty metadata.

fields.name: time_required_minutes
fields.description: Estimated time required in minutes.

fields.name: time_required_label
fields.description: Display label such as 'Under 2 hours'.

fields.name: cost_estimate
fields.description: Estimated project cost in dollars.

fields.name: cost_label
fields.description: Display label such as 'Under $100'.

fields.name: style_tags
fields.description: Tags describing decor style, theme, etc.

fields.name: steps
fields.description: Ordered list of step descriptions and images.

fields.name: materials
fields.description: List of required materials with quantities.

entities.name: SavedProject
entities.description: DIY projects saved into the My Projects list.

entities.name: ColorPalette
entities.description: Color palette inspiration cards with swatches and tags.

fields.name: tags
fields.description: Palette tags such as 'Sage Green', 'Gold'.

fields.name: swatches
fields.description: Array of swatch objects (e.g., hex code, name, position).

entities.name: Moodboard
entities.description: User-created moodboards aggregating color palettes and inspiration.

fields.name: palette_ids
fields.description: IDs of ColorPalettes added to this moodboard.

fields.name: thumbnail_image
fields.description: Optional composed thumbnail for the moodboard.

entities.name: ChecklistTemplate
entities.description: Configurable wedding planning checklists keyed by timeline, budget, and guest count.

fields.name: timeline_months
fields.description: Length of planning timeline in months (e.g., 6).

fields.name: budget_label
fields.description: Display label such as 'Under $15,000'.

fields.name: guest_count_label
fields.description: Display label such as '80–120 guests'.

entities.name: ChecklistTaskTemplate
entities.description: Individual checklist tasks grouped under checklist templates and sections.

fields.name: checklist_template_id
fields.description: Parent ChecklistTemplate ID.

fields.name: section_name
fields.description: Section label such as 'This Month', 'Month 1', etc.

fields.name: order
fields.description: Position of the task within its section.

entities.name: ToDo
entities.description: Centralized task list aggregating tasks added from checklists or manually.

fields.name: source_type
fields.description: Origin of the task.

fields.name: source_id
fields.description: ID of ChecklistTaskTemplate when source_type is 'checklist_task'.

fields.name: section_name
fields.description: Copied from checklist section (e.g., 'This Month') if applicable.

entities.name: BudgetCategory
entities.description: Fixed budget categories such as Catering, Venue, Decor used in the Budget Planner.

entities.name: BudgetCategoryAllocation
entities.description: Per-category planned vs. actual budget values displayed in the Budget Planner.

fields.name: planned_amount
fields.description: Planned budget for this category.

fields.name: actual_amount
fields.description: Actual or current estimated spend for this category.

entities.name: BudgetArticleLink
entities.description: References from the Budget Planner to helpful budget articles for a specific category (e.g., Catering).

fields.name: average_cost_per_guest
fields.description: Cached value from the article when relevant (e.g., catering cost per guest).
